notes: Every case is executed through its public evaluator. Preferred and inferior inputs are described independently of the observed score. If a case does not rank higher, exceptionReview must record both observed values and a non-empty review rationale.

comparisons.rationale: A sourced beneficial ability should outrank a sourced detrimental ability.

comparisons.preferred: Water Absorb rating and sourced Water switch-in
comparisons.inferior: Air Lock without a represented team capability
comparisons.rationale: A represented immunity and absorption should add concrete defensive value.

comparisons.preferred: Choice Band on four physical attacks
comparisons.inferior: Iron Ball on the same fast attacker
comparisons.rationale: Compatible physical amplification should outrank a sourced Speed penalty.

comparisons.preferred: Leftovers on an invested defensive build
comparisons.inferior: Eviolite on the same final evolution
comparisons.rationale: Usable recovery should outrank mitigation whose sourced evolution condition is false.
Repeatable pressure, coverage, pivoting, and speed control are the stronger package.
A build should use its invested attacking stat.
Setup should count only when attacks exploit the boost.
Distinct coverage reaches more relevant targets than redundant attacks.
A bulky pivot needs pressure and movement as well as sustain.
Setting and removing hazards are separate useful team jobs.
Screens create team value while attacks keep the setter active.
Mutually exclusive weather setters cannot be active together.

comparisons.preferred: one setup move with three payoffs
comparisons.rationale: A usable setup line needs attacks rather than redundant boosts.

comparisons.preferred: status and recovery on a bulky build
comparisons.rationale: A defensive build benefits from sustain and disruption.
Distinct utility jobs form a more coherent package than repeated coverage.

comparisons.preferred: accurate dual STAB, coverage, and removal
comparisons.rationale: Repeatable progress plus removal outweighs avoidable accuracy risk.

comparisons.inferior: same roster with passive unsupported packages
comparisons.rationale: A roster covering style jobs should outrank one stripped of pressure and a closer.

comparisons.preferred: generated roster with a proactive closer
comparisons.inferior: same roster without attacks or setup payoff
comparisons.rationale: A concrete path to closing is preferable to a passive roster.

comparisons.inferior: same attackers with no speed control
comparisons.rationale: Natural Speed is an observable plan for handling faster threats.

comparisons.inferior: same physical bulk with weak special bulk
comparisons.rationale: Independent resilience on both damage axes avoids an exploitable one-sided plan.

comparisons.preferred: pivot, screens, setup, hazards, removal, and mixed pressure
comparisons.rationale: Cross-member interactions count only when their concrete prerequisites and payoffs coexist.
Individual strength alone should not manufacture synergy.

comparisons.preferred: the same useful roster available Early
comparisons.inferior: the same useful roster available Late
comparisons.rationale: Earlier access improves journey usability without changing final-team builds.

comparisons.preferred: Early and Mid contributors before two Late additions
comparisons.rationale: A progressively functional roster is usable before the end of the journey.
